Responsive to player input; collecting power-ups; progressing through threat landscape

notes: Gynoug operates within a classic good-vs-evil shmup framework. Visual communication relies heavily on genre conventions: angel protagonist vs. demonic enemies, power-up collection mechanics for temporary advantage, and progressive difficulty escalation representing world-state deterioration toward final confrontation. The game's manifestation layer is minimal in Wikipedia source but typical for 1991 shmup design—enemy behavior, visual density, and boss encounters serve as primary world-state indicators. Noriyuki Iwadare's composition likely reinforces mood shifts (threat escalation through audio). Limited development documentation prevents detailed analysis of intentional visual semiotics beyond plot summary.
